equivalence_argument: No equivalent public service module or ExecutorRegistryService export exists in the audited repos.

negative_search_evidence.missing_behavior: The crate root does not publicly expose a service module or ExecutorRegistryService, so downstream users cannot import the full API named by the invariant.
related_but_insufficient_code: crates/ritual-spc-verification/src/lib.rs re-exports ExecutorRegistry, ExecutorRegistryConfig, ExecutorRegistryStats, and ExecutorInfo, but it declares no service module and exports no ExecutorRegistryService.
crates/ritual-spc-verification/src has verifier.rs, lib.rs, types.rs, error.rs, and client.rs; service.rs is absent.

notes: Registry types remain public, but the invariant also requires service and ExecutorRegistryService exports.
rationale: The commit title explicitly says these service module and executor registry types are exported in the public API, and the diff changes crate-root `pub mod`/`pub use` declarations. No candidate in this bounded assignment contradicts or removes it.
statement: Downstream users of `ritual-spc-verification` can import `service`, `ExecutorRegistryService`, and the executor registry types directly from the crate root after this commit.
suggested_falsification_tests: Add or keep a downstream compile test that imports `ritual_spc_verification::{service, ExecutorRegistryService, ExecutorRegistry, ExecutorRegistryConfig, ExecutorRegistryStats, ExecutorInfo}` from the crate root and fails if any item is no longer public there.
summary: Executor registry service APIs are not public because the service symbol is absent.
test_or_falsification_candidate: Add a downstream compile test importing ritual_spc_verification::{service, ExecutorRegistryService, ExecutorRegistry, ExecutorRegistryConfig, ExecutorRegistryStats, ExecutorInfo}; it should currently fail on service and ExecutorRegistryService.
title: Executor registry APIs remain public
